27	// Exit codes for ddx try terminal outcomes. These are specific to ddx try
28	// and intentionally do not reuse the generic ExitCode* constants because
29	// the bead-execution outcome vocabulary is distinct from generic CLI errors.
30	const (
31		tryExitSuccess         = 0 // success or already_satisfied
32		tryExitPreservedOrNone = 1 // preserved_for_review or no_changes
33		tryExitFailed          = 2 // execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
34	)
35	
36	// newTryCommand creates the top-level "ddx try <bead-id>" command.
37	// It targets a single specific bead through the full production execution
38	// machinery (claim → execute → land → review), unlike "ddx work" which drains
39	// the queue by picker priority and cannot force a specific bead.
40	func (f *CommandFactory) newTryCommand() *cobra.Command {
41		cmd := &cobra.Command{
42			Use:          "try <bead-id>",
43			Short:        "Execute a specific bead through the production machinery",
44			SilenceUsage: true,
45			Long: `try executes a single named bead through the full production
46	execution machinery: claim → execute → land → review.
47	
48	Unlike "ddx work" which drains the queue by picker priority, "ddx try"
49	targets a specific bead ID. This is useful for:
50	  - Operator-driven debugging when a bead misbehaves under autonomous drain
51	  - Deterministic queue ordering during refactor execution (dispatch each child
52	    in dep order with an explicit ID)
53	  - Path-comparison studies that need to execute one fixture bead at a time
54	
55	Pre-flight checks (run before any claim is issued):
56	  - bead not found → non-zero exit with "bead not found: <id>"
57	  - bead not claimable (status=closed, cancelled, etc.) → non-zero exit
58	  - bead has unmet dependencies → non-zero exit listing the blockers
59	
60	Review is on by default. --no-review is a break-glass override and
61	requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing. A bead label of review:skip
62	is only honored when it also carries a sibling review:skip-reason:*
63	label; otherwise the label is ignored.
64	
65	Flag plumbing is identical to "ddx work": --harness, --model, --provider,
66	--profile, --effort are forwarded to the agent as opaque passthrough
67	constraints. DDx does not validate or branch on their string values.
68	
69	Exit codes:
70	  0  success or already_satisfied
71	  1  preserved_for_review or no_changes
72	  2  execution_failed or any other terminal non-success
73	`,
74			Example: `  # Execute a specific bead
75	  ddx try ddx-abc12345
76	
77	  # Execute with an explicit harness and model
78	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --harness codex --model gpt-5.4-mini
79	
80	  # Execute without the post-merge review step (break-glass only)
81	  ddx try ddx-abc12345 --no-review --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing`,
82			Args: cobra.ExactArgs(1),
83			RunE: f.runTry,
84		}
85	
86		// Routing knobs — identical to "ddx work" passthrough surface.
87		cmd.Flags().String("project", "", "Target project root path or name (default: CWD git root). Env: DDX_PROJECT_ROOT")
88		cmd.Flags().String("from", "", "Base git revision to start from (default: HEAD)")
89		cmd.Flags().String("harness", "", "Agent harness constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
90		cmd.Flags().String("model", "", "Model constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
91		cmd.Flags().String("profile", "", "Routing profile: default, cheap, fast, or smart (empty = unconstrained; let the agent service choose)")
92		cmd.Flags().String("provider", "", "Provider constraint (passthrough; ddx try does not validate)")
93		cmd.Flags().String("effort", "", "Effort level")
94		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review", false, "Skip post-merge review (break-glass: requires --no-review-i-know-what-im-doing)")
95		cmd.Flags().Bool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing", false, "Break-glass acknowledgement required when using --no-review")
96		cmd.Flags().String("review-harness", "", "Harness for the post-merge reviewer (default: same as implementation harness)")
97		cmd.Flags().String("review-model", "", "Model override for the post-merge reviewer (default: smart tier)")
98		cmd.Flags().Duration("request-timeout", 0, "Per-request provider wall-clock timeout; overrides project config and model-class defaults")
99		cmd.Flags().Int("min-power", 0, "Minimum model power required (0 = unconstrained)")
100		cmd.Flags().Int("max-power", 0, "Maximum model power allowed (0 = unconstrained)")
101	
102		return cmd
103	}

105	// runTry is the RunE for "ddx try <bead-id>".
106	func (f *CommandFactory) runTry(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
107		if err := f.checkLifecycleMigrationGate(cmd); err != nil {
108			return err
109		}
110	
111		beadID := args[0]
112	
113		projectFlag, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("project")
114		projectRoot := resolveProjectRoot(projectFlag, f.WorkingDir)
115	
116		// Preflight: warn once per process for degraded project-local skill layout.
117		// Runs before claim/worktree setup so the operator sees the warning before

119		preflightResult := checkProjectRuntimePreflight(projectRoot)
120		f.preflightWarnOnce.Do(func() {
121			emitPreflightWarning(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), preflightResult)
122		})

124		fromRev, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("from")
125		harness, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("harness")
126		model, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("model")
127		profile, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("profile")
128		provider, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("provider")
129		effort, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("effort")
130		noReview, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review")
131		noReviewAck, _ := cmd.Flags().GetBool("no-review-i-know-what-im-doing")

133		reviewModel, _ := cmd.Flags().GetString("review-model")

459			ResourceChecker:       resourceChecker,
460			SessionID:             loopSessionID,
461			PreClaimHook:          buildCLIPreClaimHook(projectRoot, cliLandingOps),
462			PreDispatchLintHook:   lintHook,
463			PostAttemptTriageHook: triageHook,
464			NoReview:              noReview,
465		})
466		if runErr != nil {
467			if (errors.Is(runErr, context.Canceled) || errors.Is(runErr, context.DeadlineExceeded)) && result != nil && len(result.Results) > 0 {
468				writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), result.Results[0])
469			}
470			return runErr
471		}
472	
473		// Report outcome.
474		if result.NoReadyWork {
475			// Bead was not in the execution-ready queue even after pre-flight passed.
476			// This can happen if a heartbeat re-appeared just before claim, making it
477			// not stale-enough for reclaim. Surface a clear message.
478			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.ErrOrStderr(), "bead is not execution-ready (it may be actively claimed by another worker): %s\n", beadID)
479			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
480		}
481	
482		if len(result.Results) == 0 {
483			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
484		}
485		report := result.Results[0]
486		writeTryResult(cmd.OutOrStdout(), report)
487		if intent := escalation.ParseExecutionHint(target); intent.Source == escalation.ExecutionIntentSourceBeadHint && intent.RequestedTier != "" {
488			fmt.Fprintf(cmd.OutOrStdout(), "routing intent: tier=%s source=%s\n", intent.RequestedTier, intent.Source)
489		}
490	
491		return tryExitCodeForStatus(report.Status)
492	}

494	func writeTryResult(w io.Writer, report agent.ExecuteBeadReport) {
495		fmt.Fprintf(w, "\nbead: %s\nstatus: %s\n", report.BeadID, report.Status)
496		if report.Detail != "" {
497			fmt.Fprintf(w, "detail: %s\n", report.Detail)
498		}
499		if report.ResultRev != "" {
500			fmt.Fprintf(w, "result_rev: %s\n", report.ResultRev)
501		}
502		if report.PreserveRef != "" {
503			fmt.Fprintf(w, "preserve_ref: %s\n", report.PreserveRef)
504		}
505	}

507	// tryExitCodeForStatus maps a bead execution status string to the ddx try exit code contract.
508	func tryExitCodeForStatus(status string) error {
509		switch status {
510		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied:
511			return nil // exit 0
512		case agent.ExecuteBeadStatusPreservedNeedsReview, agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
513			agent.ExecuteBeadStatusNoEvidenceProduced:
514			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitPreservedOrNone, Message: ""}
515		default:
516			// execution_failed and all other terminal non-success statuses
517			return &ExitError{Code: tryExitFailed, Message: ""}
518		}
519	}

521	// unmetDeps returns the IDs of deps that are not yet closed, in the order
522	// they appear in the bead's dependency list.
523	func unmetDeps(store *bead.Store, b *bead.Bead) []string {
524		depIDs := b.DepIDs()
525		if len(depIDs) == 0 {
526			return nil
527		}
528		var unmet []string
529		for _, depID := range depIDs {
530			dep, err := store.Get(context.Background(), depID)
531			if err != nil || dep.Status != bead.StatusClosed {
532				unmet = append(unmet, depID)
533			}
534		}
535		return unmet
536	}

538	// singleBeadStore wraps a *bead.Store and overrides ReadyExecution to return
539	// only a single target bead. All other operations are delegated unchanged.
540	// This allows the execute-bead worker to treat the forced bead as if it were
541	// the only entry in the execution-ready queue.
542	type singleBeadStore struct {
543		*bead.Store
544		target bead.Bead
545	}
546	
547	func (s *singleBeadStore) ReadyExecution() ([]bead.Bead, error) {
548		return []bead.Bead{s.target}, nil
549	}

119

121		go func() {

23	// ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime carries the non-serializable plumbing and
24	// per-invocation runtime intent for an execute-bead loop run. Durable knobs
25	// (assignee, retry caps, harness/model, tier bounds, etc.) live on
26	// config.ResolvedConfig and are passed via Run's rcfg argument.
27	type ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime struct {
28		Log             io.Writer
29		CleanupLog      io.Writer
30		CleanupRunner   executionCleanupRunner
31		ResourceChecker ExecutionResourceChecker
32		CleanupInterval time.Duration
33		CleanupTickCh   <-chan time.Time
34		EventSink       io.Writer
35		ProgressCh      chan<- ProgressEvent
36		PreClaimHook    func(ctx context.Context) error
37		// PreClaimIntakeHook runs after routing preflight and before Claim. It
38		// classifies the candidate for actionability/scope; only
39		// actionable_atomic proceeds directly to Claim. Non-atomic outcomes
40		// skip the bead without claim. intake_error fail-opens so infrastructure
41		// failures do not become hidden dispatch failures.
42		PreClaimIntakeHook    PreClaimIntakeHook
43		PreDispatchLintHook   func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (LintResult, error)
44		PostAttemptTriageHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string, report ExecuteBeadReport) (TriageResult, error)
45		// PostAttemptDecompositionHook is called when a no_changes attempt signals
46		// orchestrator_action: decompose in its rationale, indicating the bead is too
47		// large for implementation-level splitting. The hook should run the same
48		// orchestrator splitter used by the intake gate and return child specs and an
49		// AC map. A nil return or error falls back to operator-required parking.
50		PostAttemptDecompositionHook func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (*PreClaimDecomposition, error)
51		// ReviewCostCap, when non-nil, accumulates reviewer cost on the same
52		// loop budget tracker used by the implementer attempts.
53		ReviewCostCap *escalation.CostCapTracker
54		// RoutePreflight, when non-nil, runs once during Run bootstrap before
55		// the drain loop starts. It is expected to call upstream ResolveRoute
56		// against the loop's resolved (harness, model) and return whatever typed
57		// routing error the upstream surfaced — notably
58		// agent.ErrHarnessModelIncompatible when the harness allow-list rejects
59		// the model. Any non-nil error stops the worker before any bead is
60		// claimed; DDx does NOT duplicate the upstream allow-list.
61		RoutePreflight func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error
62		// BudgetStop, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next bead. It
63		// lets CLI/server callers surface already-tripped budget state as a typed
64		// work-layer StopCondition before any new bead is claimed. The companion
65		// report is still appended for operator visibility, but the stop itself is
66		// classified through work.StopConditionBudget.
67		BudgetStop func() (work.StopDecision, ExecuteBeadReport, bool)
68		// BinaryRefreshCheck, when non-nil, is checked before selecting the next
69		// bead. A true result means the caller has started a replacement worker
70		// with equivalent arguments and this loop should exit before claiming work.
71		// Errors are logged and fail open so update-probing never blocks the queue.
72		BinaryRefreshCheck func(ctx context.Context) (bool, error)
73		// Mode and IdleInterval are the runtime loop intent. Once and
74		// PollInterval remain for older tests/callers but production entry points
75		// should set Mode and IdleInterval directly.
76		Mode         executeloop.Mode
77		IdleInterval time.Duration
78		Once         bool
79		PollInterval time.Duration
80		NoReview     bool

func TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauProfileByMetadata(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("DDX_DISABLE_UPDATE_CHECK", "1")
	stub := installExecuteCapturingStub(t)
	stub.listPolicies, stub.listModels = canonicalFizeauPolicyFixture()
	stub.executeFn = func(req agentlib.ServiceExecuteRequest) (<-chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, error) {
		ch := make(chan agentlib.ServiceEvent, 1)
		ch <- agentlib.ServiceEvent{Type: "final", Data: []byte(`{"status":"success","final_text":"{\"classification\":\"ready\",\"rationale\":\"ok\",\"readiness_checks\":[],\"score\":9,\"suggested_fixes\":[],\"waivers_applied\":[],\"recommended_action\":\"release_claim_retry\",\"suggested_amendments\":[],\"suggested_followup_beads\":[]}"}`)}
		close(ch)
		return ch, nil
	}

	dir := minimalProjectDir(t)
	require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(dir, "README.md"), []byte("# test\n"), 0o644))
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "init", dir).Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.email", "test@example.com").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "config", "user.name", "Test User").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "add", ".").Run())
	require.NoError(t, exec.Command("git", "-C", dir, "commit", "-m", "init").Run())
	store := bead.NewStore(filepath.Join(dir, ".ddx"))
	require.NoError(t, store.Init())
	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-work-tier-standard",
		Title:     "Work with inferred standard routing tier",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	factory := NewCommandFactory(dir)
	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", dir,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",
	)

	requests := capturedImplementationRequests(stub)
	require.NotEmpty(t, requests, "ddx work must invoke implementation dispatch; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	lastReq := requests[0]
	assert.Equal(t, "default", lastReq.Policy, "work should request the ordinary no-requirement Fizeau policy by metadata")
	assert.Equal(t, 7, lastReq.MinPower, "work should use the selected policy floor instead of escalating to the strongest profile")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Harness, "zero-config work must not hard-pin a harness")
	assert.Empty(t, lastReq.Provider, "zero-config work must not hard-pin a provider")

		} else {

	require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
		ID:        "ddx-zero-config-work-tier-retry",
		Title:     "Work retries within the selected policy at MinPower=actual+1",
		IssueType: "bug",
	}))

	root := factory.NewRootCommand()
	out, err := executeCommand(
		root,
		"work",
		"--once",
		"--project", dir,
		"--no-review",
		"--no-review-i-know-what-im-doing",

	require.Len(t, requests, 2, "ddx work should retry an escalatable implementation failure; output=%q err=%v", out, err)
	assert.Equal(t, "default", requests[0].Policy, "first attempt should use the inferred no-requirement default policy")
	assert.Equal(t, 0, requests[0].MinPower, "first attempt must not send an initial MinPower floor")
	assert.Equal(t, "default", requests[1].Policy, "retry should stay within the selected policy band")

}

DDx currently duplicates Fizeau policy intent with numeric power on initial zero-config implementation dispatch. In the current code path, DDx selects the `default` Fizeau policy for ordinary implementation work and then also sets `MinPower` to that policy floor. That makes the request shape `Policy=default, MinPower=7` instead of letting Fizeau fully interpret the selected policy.

ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE
`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:396-401` selects an implementation profile and raises `initialMinPower` to `selection.MinPower` on the first attempt. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:426-442` then passes `requestedMinPower` into `singleTierAttempt`, which writes it into `config.CLIOverrides.MinPower` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:289-294`. `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258` sends both `Policy` and `MinPower` to Fizeau. The same first-attempt pattern exists for `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:293-322`. Current command tests assert this duplicated shape in `cli/cmd/work_test.go` and `cli/cmd/try_test.go`, so the tests encode the wrong contract.

DESIRED ROUTING CONTRACT
For initial zero-config implementation work, DDx should select an opaque Fizeau policy by metadata and send only that policy. Example: ordinary work selects `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`. Fizeau owns the model choice and may choose a lower-power model inside that policy if the policy permits it.

For implementation fallback escalation, DDx should keep policy intent and add a numeric floor only after evidence of failure. Example: DDx selects `Policy=default`; Fizeau chooses a power-5 model; the attempt fails; DDx retries with `Policy=default, MinPower=6`. If repeated failures or ladder exhaustion require moving policy bands, DDx may reselect the appropriate policy by metadata, but numeric floors are evidence-driven retry constraints, not initial policy duplication.

For review routing, DDx may use `MinPower` immediately when the reviewer must be stronger than the implementation route or when review pairing evidence requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
Change zero-config implementation routing so first attempts do not copy the selected policy floor into `MinPower`. Preserve explicit operator `--min-power`, bead numeric tier hints, reviewer escalation, and retry escalation. On retry, derive the next floor from the actual route power when available; otherwise use the selected policy floor/ladder only as an escalation basis, not as an initial dispatch floor.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Fizeau policy names. Do not add DDx-side provider/model selection. Do not change Fizeau policy definitions. Do not remove MinPower support for explicit user flags, review escalation, or implementation retry escalation.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts ordinary zero-config `ddx try` sends `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, and no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture.
2. `TestWorkZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts ordinary zero-config `ddx work` sends `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, and no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture.
3. `TestWorkZeroConfigRetryAddsMinPowerFloorWithinSelectedPolicy` asserts a failed first implementation attempt with actual power 5 retries with `Policy=default` and `MinPower=6` rather than sending an initial floor.
4. Existing review escalation tests still prove reviewer dispatch can send both policy and MinPower when review pairing or review retry requires it.
5. `cd cli && go test ./cmd/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestTryZeroConfig.*MinPower|TestWorkZeroConfig.*MinPower|Test.*Review.*MinPower|TestSelectImplementationProfile" -count=1` passes.

DDx currently duplicates Fizeau policy intent with numeric power on initial zero-config implementation dispatch. In the current code path, DDx selects the `default` Fizeau policy for ordinary implementation work and then also sets `MinPower` to that policy floor. That makes the request shape `Policy=default, MinPower=7` instead of letting Fizeau fully interpret the selected policy.

`cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:396-401` selects an implementation profile and raises `initialMinPower` to `selection.MinPower` on the first attempt. `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:426-442` then passes `requestedMinPower` into `singleTierAttempt`, which writes it into `config.CLIOverrides.MinPower` at `cli/cmd/execute_loop_shared.go:289-294`. `cli/internal/agent/service_run.go:241-258` sends both `Policy` and `MinPower` to Fizeau. The same first-attempt pattern exists for `ddx try` at `cli/cmd/try.go:293-322`. Current command tests assert this duplicated shape in `cli/cmd/work_test.go` and `cli/cmd/try_test.go`, so the tests encode the wrong contract.

DESIRED ROUTING CONTRACT
For initial zero-config implementation work, DDx should select an opaque Fizeau policy by metadata and send only that policy. Example: ordinary work selects `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`. Fizeau owns the model choice and may choose a lower-power model inside that policy if the policy permits it.

For implementation fallback escalation, DDx should keep policy intent and add a numeric floor only after evidence of failure. Example: DDx selects `Policy=default`; Fizeau chooses a power-5 model; the attempt fails; DDx retries with `Policy=default, MinPower=6`. If repeated failures or ladder exhaustion require moving policy bands, DDx may reselect the appropriate policy by metadata, but numeric floors are evidence-driven retry constraints, not initial policy duplication.

For review routing, DDx may use `MinPower` immediately when the reviewer must be stronger than the implementation route or when review pairing evidence requires it.

PROPOSED FIX
Change zero-config implementation routing so first attempts do not copy the selected policy floor into `MinPower`. Preserve explicit operator `--min-power`, bead numeric tier hints, reviewer escalation, and retry escalation. On retry, derive the next floor from the actual route power when available; otherwise use the selected policy floor/ladder only as an escalation basis, not as an initial dispatch floor.

NON-SCOPE
Do not hard-code Fizeau policy names. Do not add DDx-side provider/model selection. Do not change Fizeau policy definitions. Do not remove MinPower support for explicit user flags, review escalation, or implementation retry escalation.     </description>     <acceptance> 1. `TestTryZeroConfigInferredTaskSelectsFizeauPolicyWithoutInitialMinPower` asserts ordinary zero-config `ddx try` sends `Policy=default`, `MinPower=0`, and no harness/provider/model pins under the canonical Fizeau fixture.
